purpose: Governed data product contract that defines ownership, access, SLA, quality, sensitivity, refresh, and API expectations for the Global HR metadata-first platform.
global_principles: Metadata-first design: JSON contracts are the source of truth for documentation, build sequencing, security, and integration planning.
Domain ownership: each HR domain has business and technical accountability.
Security by design: access is granted by department, domain, role, and data sensitivity.
Quality as a contract: every load must satisfy uniqueness, null, referential integrity, and row-count checks.
API-ready architecture: every governed table should be consumable through Dataverse/OData-style APIs.

global_quality_rules.rule: Primary keys must be unique and not null.
Required Dataverse columns must not be null.
Foreign key / lookup values must exist in parent tables before child load.
Loaded row counts must reconcile to source row counts within threshold.
Restricted and confidential fields require field security review.
Incremental load columns must be validated for date continuity when present.

data_products.name: Global HR - Absence Management
data_products.description: Curated data product for Absence Management reporting, analytics, Dataverse integration, and governed consumption.

refresh_policy.mode: CSV batch to Dataverse / Power BI semantic model
refresh_policy.recommended_frequency: Daily batch recommended for demo; enterprise pattern may be near-real-time or scheduled daily.

sla.availability_target: 99.5% for demo platform; 99.9%+ for production enterprise platform
sla.freshness_target: T+1 business day unless source system is configured for real-time API sync

security_contract.access_model: Team-based access using department/domain security profiles
security_contract.pii_handling: Mask, restrict, or aggregate person-level identifiers for non-authorized consumers.
security_contract.external_sharing: Not permitted unless published as anonymized demo content.

api_contract.style: REST-first; SOAP compatibility notes may be added for HRIS integrations such as Workday or SAP legacy flows.
api_contract.auth: OAuth2 / Microsoft Entra ID service principal or delegated access

implementation_alignment.build_pattern: Create tables and alternate keys first, then lookups/relationships, then security profiles, then data loads by dependency order.

approval_workflow.versioning_policy: Semantic versioning: major for contract-breaking changes, minor for new optional fields/products, patch for documentation fixes.
